_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 8-1 Downgrade of Response (Revised 12/27/2022, MDHHS Reviewed 2022). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan System Protocol Section 8-1: Downgrade of Response — MDHHS Approved 12/27/2022
purpose: Govern conditions under which an EMS response may be downgraded (reduced in priority or level) after initial dispatch. Downgrade decisions must balance scene safety, patient need, and resource availability. Medical control or dispatch authority may authorize downgrade based on additional information received after initial dispatch.

sections.title: Authority to Downgrade
points: A responding unit may reduce its response priority (lights and sirens to routine) based on additional information received after dispatch
Dispatch may authorize downgrade if additional caller information or scene information indicates a lower-acuity response is appropriate
The crew chief of the responding unit retains responsibility for ensuring downgrade is appropriate for the reported patient condition
Medical control may be contacted to authorize downgrade when clinical judgment supports it

sections.title: Conditions That May Support Downgrade
points: Caller or secondary caller information indicates patient is ambulatory, alert, and in no acute distress
Scene is confirmed as non-emergency (e.g., scheduled lift assist, routine transfer)
Law enforcement or first responders on scene confirm patient is stable with no acute need
Initial dispatch was precautionary and updated information indicates lower acuity
Medical priority dispatch protocol supports non-emergency response based on updated information

sections.title: Conditions That Do NOT Support Downgrade
points: Unconscious or unresponsive patient
Chest pain, difficulty breathing, or stroke symptoms
Cardiac arrest or suspected arrest
Pediatric emergencies
Major trauma
Any condition where delay could result in patient harm
When unable to confirm patient status via secondary caller or first responder

sections.title: Documentation
points: Document the reason for downgrade in the PCR
Document who authorized the downgrade (dispatch, crew chief, or medical control)
Document time of downgrade decision
Document any updated patient information that supported the downgrade decision

deterioration_flags: Updated scene information indicates higher acuity than initial downgrade assessment — upgrade response immediately
First responders on scene report patient deterioration after downgrade — upgrade immediately
scope_notes: Downgrade authority: dispatch, crew chief, or medical control
Never downgrade for: unconscious patient, cardiac arrest, chest pain, SOB, stroke, pediatric emergency, major trauma
Document reason, authority, and time of downgrade
Crew chief retains responsibility for appropriateness of downgrade
